articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Gains Momentum with New Investment Hubs
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced the establishment of three new Green Hydrogen production and export hubs across coastal states.
• This initiative aims to accelerate India's ambitious target of producing 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
• Private sector investment pledges worth over ₹50,000 crores were secured, focusing on electrolyser manufacturing and renewable energy integration.
• The move is expected to significantly boost India's energy security, reduce reliance on fossil fuels, and position the nation as a global leader in green energy exports.
• Skill development programs and R&D grants for hydrogen technologies were also announced to support the ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation
Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) / PIB
prelimsPoints: India's target for green hydrogen production by 2030 (5 million tonnes).
Key components of green hydrogen production (electrolysis, renewable energy sources).
Role of MNRE in promoting green energy.
Geographical focus of new hubs (coastal states).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the economic potential of India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and infrastructure in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in achieving renewable energy targets.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.area: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Renewable energy technologies, climate change mitigation

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Key Federalism Ruling
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment clarifying the legislative competence of states versus the Union in matters of concurrent list subjects.
• The ruling stemmed from a dispute over a state law regulating agricultural markets, which was challenged by the Union government.
• The Court emphasized the principle of cooperative federalism, stating that while the Union has overriding powers in certain circumstances, states retain significant autonomy.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for future legislative actions concerning subjects like education, forests, and trade.
• Experts suggest the ruling reaffirms the constitutional balance of power and strengthens the federal structure of India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
articles.source: The Hindu / Live Law
prelimsPoints: Role of Supreme Court in interpreting constitutional provisions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the Union-State relations in India.
Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism and its challenges in the Indian context.
Evaluate how judicial pronouncements shape the balance of power between the Union and states.
Examine the significance of legislative competence in maintaining the federal structure.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The 'basic structure doctrine' of the Indian Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in the Kesavananda Bharati case in 1973.

articles.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Inflation Remains a Concern
articles.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew by 7.2% in the second quarter (July-September) of FY 2025-26, surpassing market forecasts.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the manufacturing and services sectors, coupled with increased government capital expenditure.
• However, retail inflation, particularly food inflation, continued to remain elevated at 6.8%, putting pressure on household budgets.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated it would maintain a cautious monetary policy stance, prioritizing inflation control while supporting growth.
• Analysts predict continued economic resilience but highlight the need for structural reforms to address persistent inflationary pressures and ensure equitable growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major Crops - Cropping Patterns in various parts of the country, - Different Types of Irrigation and Irrigation Systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
articles.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) / RBI
prelimsPoints: Primary drivers of economic growth (manufacturing, services).
Key economic indicators and their calculation (GDP, CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust economic growth in Q2 FY 2025-26.
Discuss the challenges posed by persistent inflation and the measures taken by the RBI to control it.
Evaluate the balance between growth and inflation control in India's current economic policy framework.
Suggest structural reforms necessary to sustain economic growth and mitigate inflationary pressures in the long term.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing GDP growth and inflation trends in India.
articles.trivia: India aims to become a developed economy by 2047, the centenary of its independence.

articles.headline: India and ASEAN Deepen Maritime Security Cooperation amidst Regional Challenges
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the annual India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue, focusing on enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions centered on joint naval exercises, information sharing, and capacity building to address challenges like piracy, illegal fishing, and maritime terrorism.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for collaboration in disaster management and humanitarian assistance in the maritime domain.
• This strengthened partnership is seen as crucial for regional stability and countering growing geopolitical complexities in the South China Sea.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) / PTI
keyTerms: UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea)
prelimsPoints: Key challenges in maritime security (piracy, illegal fishing).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-ASEAN maritime cooperation for regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its strategic partnership with ASEAN.
Evaluate India's 'Act East' policy in the context of its maritime security objectives.
Examine how adherence to international law like UNCLOS contributes to a rules-based order in the maritime domain.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and ASEAN member states participating in a joint exercise.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission: India Makes Strides in Quantum Computing and Communication
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) released a progress report on the National Quantum Mission (NQM), highlighting significant advancements.
• Indian researchers have achieved breakthroughs in developing indigenous quantum processors and secure quantum communication protocols.
• New funding allocations were announced for setting up more Quantum Technology Hubs in premier academic and research institutions.
• The mission aims to propel India into the league of leading nations in quantum technology development, with applications in computing, communication, sensing, and metrology.
• Industry collaborations are being fostered to translate research into commercial applications, creating a robust quantum ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
articles.source: Department of Science & Technology (DST) / DRDO
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Quantum Mission.
Key areas of quantum technology (computing, communication, sensing).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Quantum Mission for India's technological sovereignty and economic growth.
Analyze the potential applications of quantum computing and communication in various sectors (defense, finance, healthcare).
Examine the challenges in developing a robust quantum technology ecosystem in India, including skill development and infrastructure.
Evaluate how government initiatives like NQM can foster innovation and indigenization in advanced technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract graphic representing quantum bits (qubits) and quantum entanglement.
articles.trivia: A qubit, the basic unit of quantum information, can exist in multiple states simultaneously, unlike a classical bit which is either 0 or 1.

articles.headline: Flagship Rural Development Schemes Show Positive Impact on Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Rural Development indicated significant progress in key flagship schemes.
• Programs like MGNREGA, Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), and Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) have shown enhanced outreach.
• Data revealed a noticeable improvement in rural infrastructure, employment generation, and women's self-help group empowerment.
• Challenges persist in equitable resource distribution and timely implementation in remote areas, requiring further administrative reforms.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to boosting rural incomes and reducing poverty through integrated development strategies.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Rural Development / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Nodal Ministry for rural development schemes.
Role of Self-Help Groups in women's empowerment.
Key indicators of rural development (employment, infrastructure).
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the impact of flagship rural development schemes on poverty alleviation and livelihood generation in India.
Discuss the role of women's Self-Help Groups in promoting economic empowerment and social change in rural areas.
Examine the administrative and logistical challenges in implementing large-scale rural development programs and suggest solutions.
Analyze how an integrated approach to rural development can lead to sustainable and inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: Rural women participating in a self-help group meeting in a village.
articles.trivia: MGNREGA guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.

articles.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules for Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change proposed significant amendments to the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, focusing on stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
• The amendments aim to streamline the collection, dismantling, and recycling processes, encouraging a circular economy approach for electronic goods.
• New provisions include mandatory digital manifests for e-waste movement and enhanced penalties for non-compliance by producers and recyclers.
• The move is crucial to tackle the escalating problem of electronic waste, which poses severe environmental and health hazards.
• Stakeholder consultations are underway, with industry bodies and environmental groups providing feedback on the proposed changes.
gsSubTopics: Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: Key provisions of E-Waste Management Rules.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by e-waste generation in India and the necessity of robust management policies.
Analyze how the proposed amendments to E-Waste Management Rules can promote a circular economy and sustainable consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in the Indian context for managing electronic waste.
Suggest measures to improve e-waste collection, recycling infrastructure, and public awareness in India.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets representing e-waste.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of e-waste globally, after China and the United States.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Defense and Economic Ties
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to a Special Strategic and Global Partnership.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint exercises, technology transfer, and collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
• Economic ties were also a major agenda, with talks on boosting trade, investment, and supply chain resilience between the two nations.
• Both countries underscored their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed regional security challenges.
• The dialogue also covered cooperation in infrastructure development, clean energy, and people-to-people exchanges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) / The Japan Times
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of India-Japan relations (defense, economy, strategic).
Economic initiatives between India and Japan.
Role of Quad in regional security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' with Japan in the context of changing geopolitical dynamics.
Discuss the potential for enhanced defense and economic cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India-Japan collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
Examine how bilateral partnerships like India-Japan contribute to regional stability and a rules-based international order.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India, particularly in infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Expanding, Focus on Inclusivity
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced plans to further expand India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• Initiatives include integrating more government services into the Aadhaar-UPI-DigiLocker stack and promoting digital literacy in rural areas.
• The focus is on ensuring equitable access to digital services and financial inclusion for all segments of society.
• New guidelines for data governance and privacy protection within the DPI framework are also being finalized.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving the vision of a truly digital and inclusive India, leveraging technology for public welfare.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
articles.source: MeitY / The Indian Express
prelimsPoints: Components of India's DPI (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker).
Importance of data privacy in digital services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure in promoting financial inclusion and e-governance.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring equitable access to digital services and addressing the digital divide in India.
Evaluate the importance of robust data governance and privacy frameworks for the successful expansion of DPI.
Examine how DPI can be leveraged to improve public service delivery and enhance transparency and accountability.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing interconnected digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched a new national policy framework aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture.
• The policy emphasizes the adoption of drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• Financial incentives and technological support will be provided to farmers, especially small and marginal farmers, to adapt to changing climate patterns.
• The framework also includes provisions for robust early warning systems for extreme weather events and crop insurance reforms.
• This initiative is critical for ensuring food security and sustaining agricultural productivity in the face of climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope' and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Land reforms in India.
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare / PIB
prelimsPoints: Government schemes related to agriculture and climate (e.g., PMFBY).
Challenges faced by Indian agriculture due to climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a climate-resilient agriculture policy for ensuring India's food security in the context of climate change.
Analyze the key components of the new policy framework and their potential impact on farmers' livelihoods.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing climate-smart farming practices, especially for small and marginal farmers.
Suggest technological and policy interventions to enhance agricultural productivity and adaptability to climate variability.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting healthy crops in a field, with a focus on sustainable practices.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest agricultural producers, with agriculture contributing significantly to its GDP and employing a large portion of its workforce.

articles.headline: India Unveils Draft Policy for Private Sector Participation in Space Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and Department of Space released a draft policy framework to enable private sector participation in space tourism.
• The policy aims to leverage India's growing space capabilities and attract investment for developing sub-orbital and orbital tourism ventures.
• It outlines regulatory guidelines, safety protocols, and licensing procedures for private entities keen on offering space travel experiences.
• This move aligns with India's broader space sector reforms, encouraging greater private engagement in commercial space activities.
• Experts believe this could create new economic opportunities and inspire future generations towards space exploration.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space.
articles.source: ISRO / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Role of ISRO in India's space program.
Concept of space tourism (sub-orbital vs. orbital).
Government's policy towards private sector in space.
Regulatory bodies for space activities in India (e.g., IN-SPACe).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of India venturing into the space tourism sector.
Analyze how the new policy framework aligns with India's broader space sector reforms and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
Evaluate the regulatory and safety considerations critical for developing a sustainable space tourism industry in India.
Examine the role of private sector participation in accelerating India's space capabilities and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a futuristic spacecraft taking off for space tourism.
articles.trivia: The first private company to send a crewed mission to the International Space Station was SpaceX in 2020.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies to Undergo Major Reforms for Enhanced Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a comprehensive reform package for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country.
• The reforms aim to enhance the financial autonomy, administrative efficiency, and participatory governance of municipal corporations.
• Key proposals include increasing own-source revenue generation, capacity building for urban planners, and integrating digital solutions for public services.
• The package also emphasizes greater citizen engagement through ward committees and online feedback mechanisms.
• These reforms are critical for addressing the rapid urbanization challenges and improving the quality of life in Indian cities.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Constitutional status of ULBs (74th Amendment Act).
Government initiatives for urban development (e.g., Smart Cities Mission).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of strengthening Urban Local Bodies for effective urban governance and sustainable development in India.
Analyze the proposed reforms package, focusing on its potential to enhance financial autonomy and administrative efficiency of ULBs.
Evaluate the role of citizen participation and digital solutions in improving public service delivery in urban areas.
Examine the challenges in implementing municipal reforms and suggest strategies for successful decentralization of power and finances.
articles.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with an overlay representing digital governance services.
articles.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, passed in 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies in India.

articles.headline: India-GCC Free Trade Agreement Negotiations in Advanced Stages
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have entered their final stages.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost trade and investment flows between India and the six GCC member states.
• Key areas of discussion include tariff reductions, market access for goods and services, and investment protection.
• A successful FTA is expected to provide India with greater energy security and market access, while GCC countries benefit from India's growing economy and skilled workforce.
• Both sides expressed optimism for a swift conclusion, potentially by the end of the year.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.
articles.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry / Reuters
prelimsPoints: India's trade relations with Gulf countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the GCC.
Discuss the potential impact of the FTA on India's energy security, trade balance, and investment landscape.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in negotiating and implementing a comprehensive FTA with a bloc like GCC.
Examine how economic diplomacy through FTAs can strengthen India's geopolitical influence and economic resilience.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states arranged to represent cooperation.
articles.trivia: The GCC comprises Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focus on Telemedicine
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced the expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to include advanced telemedicine services.
• The initiative aims to connect more healthcare providers and patients through a unified digital platform, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• New features include AI-powered diagnostic support, remote monitoring for chronic diseases, and secure digital prescriptions.
• This expansion is expected to significantly improve access to quality healthcare, reduce geographical barriers, and enhance efficiency.
• Data privacy and security remain paramount, with robust encryption protocols and consent-based data sharing mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: National Health Authority (NHA) / Press Information Bureau (PIB)
prelimsPoints: Importance of data privacy in healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze how the expansion of telemedicine services under ABDM can address the challenges of healthcare access in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the ethical and privacy concerns associated with digital health records and AI-powered diagnostics.
Examine the role of technology and government initiatives in achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor conducting a virtual consultation with a patient via video call.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded healthcare scheme.

articles.headline: PM Gati Shakti Master Plan Accelerates Infrastructure Project Completion
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry released a progress report on the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan, indicating accelerated project completion.
• The multi-modal connectivity platform has significantly improved coordination among 16 ministries for integrated infrastructure development.
• Over 1,000 critical infrastructure projects, including roads, railways, ports, and airports, have been mapped and are progressing efficiently.
• The plan is enhancing logistical efficiency, reducing costs, and boosting India's economic competitiveness.
• Challenges related to land acquisition and environmental clearances are being addressed through a streamlined digital approach.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
articles.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry / PIB
prelimsPoints: Key sectors covered by Gati Shakti (roads, railways, ports).
Challenges in infrastructure projects (land acquisition, clearances).
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan in revolutionizing India's infrastructure landscape.
Analyze how Gati Shakti enhances coordination among ministries and accelerates project implementation.
Evaluate the impact of improved logistical efficiency and reduced costs on India's economic competitiveness and 'Ease of Doing Business'.
Examine the challenges in executing large-scale infrastructure projects and how Gati Shakti addresses them.
articles.imageDescription: An intricate map showing interconnected infrastructure projects like roads, railways, and ports.
articles.trivia: PM Gati Shakti was launched in October 2021 with an aim to break departmental silos and bring in more holistic and integrated planning and execution of infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: India Boosts Exploration and Mining of Critical Minerals
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines announced new policy incentives and exploration targets to accelerate the discovery and extraction of critical minerals.
• Focus areas include lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements, essential for electric vehicles, renewable energy, and defense technologies.
• The government is inviting private sector participation and international collaboration for advanced exploration techniques.
• This strategic move aims to reduce India's import dependence and bolster its self-reliance in key industrial sectors.
• Environmental safeguards and sustainable mining practices are being integrated into the policy framework.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Mines / Geological Survey of India
prelimsPoints: Examples of critical minerals (lithium, cobalt, REE).
Applications of critical minerals (EVs, renewable energy).
Role of Geological Survey of India (GSI).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, energy transition, and national security.
Analyze the policy incentives and challenges in boosting domestic exploration and mining of critical minerals.
Evaluate the environmental and social impacts of mining critical minerals and the measures for sustainable extraction.
Examine how reducing import dependence on critical minerals aligns with the 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
articles.imageDescription: Various critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth samples displayed.
articles.trivia: India recently discovered significant lithium reserves in Jammu and Kashmir, which could be a game-changer for its EV industry.

articles.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Focuses on Connectivity and Trade
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the 3rd India-Central Asia Summit, bringing together leaders from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
• The summit emphasized strengthening regional connectivity through the Chabahar Port and International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
• Discussions also focused on boosting trade, investment, and cultural exchanges, with agreements signed on energy and pharmaceuticals.
• Security cooperation, particularly in counter-terrorism and regional stability, remained a key agenda item.
• India reiterated its commitment to a long-term strategic partnership with Central Asian nations, leveraging historical ties and shared interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) / ANI
prelimsPoints: Member countries of Central Asia targeted by India.
Strategic importance of Chabahar Port and INSTC.
Key areas of cooperation (connectivity, trade, security).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India, particularly in terms of connectivity, energy security, and regional stability.
Discuss how initiatives like Chabahar Port and INSTC are crucial for enhancing India's trade and economic engagement with Central Asian countries.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in strengthening security cooperation between India and Central Asian nations, especially in counter-terrorism.
Examine India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its role in fostering a multi-faceted partnership.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) route.
articles.trivia: The International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) is a 7,200-km long multi-modal network of ship, rail, and road route for moving freight between India, Iran, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia and Europe.

articles.headline: National Research Foundation Becomes Operational, Boosts R&D Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The National Research Foundation (NRF), established under the National Education Policy 2020, has officially become operational.
• NRF will fund high-impact research across various disciplines, fostering a culture of scientific inquiry and innovation in India.
• It aims to bridge the gap between academic research and industry needs, promoting translational research and startup ecosystems.
• The foundation will prioritize interdisciplinary projects, international collaborations, and capacity building for young researchers.
• This move is expected to significantly enhance India's global standing in scientific research and technological advancement.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Education / Press Trust of India (PTI)
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the National Research Foundation in transforming India's research and development landscape.
Analyze how NRF aligns with the objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 to foster a culture of innovation.
Evaluate the potential impact of NRF on bridging the gap between academic research and industrial application.
Examine the challenges in promoting interdisciplinary research and international collaboration in India and how NRF addresses them.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized graphic representing research, innovation, and collaboration in science.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level with 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) by 2030.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Cybersecurity Framework Amidst Rising Digital Threats
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced new measures to strengthen India's cybersecurity framework.
• The initiatives include mandatory security audits for critical infrastructure, enhanced threat intelligence sharing, and public-private partnerships.
• A new national incident response team with specialized capabilities in AI and machine learning for threat detection was also unveiled.
• This move comes in response to the growing sophistication of cyberattacks targeting government systems and financial institutions.
• Public awareness campaigns on cyber hygiene and data protection are being intensified across the country.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
articles.source: NCIIPC / CERT-In
keyTerms: NCIIPC (National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre)
prelimsPoints: Importance of public-private partnerships in cybersecurity.
Measures for data protection (audits, threat intelligence).
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key components of India's strengthened cybersecurity framework and their effectiveness in protecting critical infrastructure.
Evaluate the challenges in building a robust cybersecurity ecosystem, including skill development and international cooperation.
Examine the role of individuals, private sector, and government in fostering a culture of cyber hygiene and resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a map of India, representing cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: The first major cyber attack on India's critical infrastructure was reportedly the 'Stuxnet' worm, though its impact on India was limited compared to Iran.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Shows Progress in River Rejuvenation and Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) released a progress report on the Namami Gange programme, highlighting significant achievements.
• Over 300 projects related to sewage infrastructure, industrial pollution abatement, and ghat development have been completed.
• Visible improvements in water quality at several stretches of the river have been observed due to enhanced treatment capacities.
• Public participation through 'Ganga Praharis' and awareness campaigns has played a crucial role in conservation efforts.
• Challenges persist in managing diffuse pollution sources and ensuring sustained maintenance of assets, requiring continued focus.
gsSubTopics: Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: Types of projects undertaken (sewage, industrial, ghats).
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the achievements and challenges of the Namami Gange Programme in rejuvenating the River Ganga.
Discuss the multi-faceted approach adopted by NMCG to address pollution and promote conservation along the Ganga basin.
Analyze the importance of public participation and community engagement in large-scale environmental conservation initiatives.
Suggest sustainable strategies for long-term maintenance of water quality and ecological health of the Ganga river system.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of River Ganga with ghats and people engaged in activities.
articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populated river basin in the world, supporting over 500 million people.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Dekho Apna Desh' 2.0 to Boost Domestic and International Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled 'Dekho Apna Desh' 2.0, an upgraded campaign to promote both domestic and international tourism in India.
• The initiative focuses on showcasing India's diverse cultural heritage, natural beauty, and adventure tourism potential.
• New digital platforms, immersive virtual reality experiences, and collaborations with travel influencers are key components.
• Special emphasis is being placed on developing sustainable tourism practices and promoting local economies in tourist destinations.
• The campaign aims to achieve significant growth in tourist arrivals and boost the hospitality sector, aligning with economic recovery goals.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.

Distribution of Key Natural Resources in South Asia and Indian subcontinent; factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).
articles.source: Ministry of Tourism / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Types of tourism promoted (cultural, natural, adventure).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of tourism in promoting India's cultural heritage and fostering economic growth.
Analyze the strategies employed in 'Dekho Apna Desh' 2.0 to attract both domestic and international tourists.
Evaluate the challenges in developing sustainable tourism practices and managing the environmental impact of tourism.
Examine how tourism can contribute to local economies and job creation, especially in remote and rural areas.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of iconic Indian tourist destinations like Taj Mahal, Himalayas, and a beach.
articles.trivia: The World Tourism Organization (UNWTO) is the United Nations agency responsible for the promotion of responsible, sustainable and universally accessible tourism.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy for Integrating Technology in Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education introduced a comprehensive policy framework for the integration of cutting-edge technology into the education system.
• The policy focuses on leveraging AI, virtual reality (VR), and personalized learning platforms to enhance teaching and learning outcomes.
• It mandates digital literacy training for teachers and students across all levels of education, from primary to higher education.
• Initiatives include development of open-source educational resources and deployment of smart classrooms in underserved regions.
• The aim is to create a future-ready workforce and bridge the digital divide in education, aligning with National Education Policy 2020.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Education / The Economic Times
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of technology, particularly AI and VR, in transforming the Indian education system.
Analyze the key provisions of the new policy for integrating technology in education and its alignment with NEP 2020.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing EdTech solutions across diverse educational settings, including issues of access and teacher training.
Examine how technology can help bridge the digital divide and ensure equitable access to quality education in India.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom using tablets and interactive displays.
articles.trivia: India has one of the largest education systems in the world, with over 250 million students enrolled in schools.

highlights: India's Green Hydrogen Mission accelerates with new investment hubs, targeting 5 million tonnes production by 2030.
Supreme Court upholds states' rights in a landmark federalism ruling, clarifying Union-State legislative competence.
India's Q2 GDP growth exceeds expectations at 7.2%, but retail inflation remains a concern at 6.8%.
India and ASEAN deepen maritime security cooperation, emphasizing a free and open Indo-Pacific.
National Quantum Mission progresses with breakthroughs in quantum computing and communication, boosting India's R&D ecosystem.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. India aims to produce 10 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
2. The mission primarily focuses on promoting the use of grey hydrogen in industrial applications.
3. Electrolysers are a key technology for green hydrogen production.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. India's ambitious target under the National Green Hydrogen Mission is to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, not 10 million tonnes. Statement 2 is incorrect. The mission focuses on 'green' hydrogen, produced using renewable energy, not 'grey' hydrogen, which is produced from fossil fuels. Statement 3 is correct. Electrolysers are crucial devices used to split water into hydrogen and oxygen using electricity from renewable sources, which is central to green hydrogen production.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for implementing the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)?
options: (a) Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
(d) Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the apex body responsible for implementing India’s flagship public health insurance scheme, Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY), and has also been entrusted with the responsibility of implementing the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan:
1. It aims to improve multi-modal connectivity and logistics efficiency.
2. It involves coordination among 16 ministries for integrated infrastructure development.
3. It focuses exclusively on road and railway projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. PM Gati Shakti's primary objective is to improve multi-modal connectivity and logistics efficiency across the country. Statement 2 is correct. The plan is a digital platform that brings together 16 ministries (including Railways, Roads, Ports, Shipping, Aviation, etc.) for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects. Statement 3 is incorrect. While roads and railways are major components, the plan also covers other infrastructure sectors like ports, airports, waterways, and logistics infrastructure.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a member country of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) is a regional intergovernmental political and economic union consisting of all Arab states of the Persian Gulf, except Iraq. Its members are Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates. Iran is not a member of the GCC.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the E-Waste (Management) Rules in India, consider the following:
1. Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is a key principle of these rules.
2. The rules aim to promote a circular economy for electronic goods.
3. The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is the nodal ministry.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The E-Waste (Management) Rules in India are based on the principle of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), making producers responsible for the end-of-life management of their products (Statement 1). The rules are designed to streamline e-waste collection, dismantling, and recycling processes to encourage a circular economy approach (Statement 2). The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is indeed the nodal ministry for environmental regulations, including e-waste management (Statement 3).

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving energy security and mitigating climate change. What are the key challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production in India?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Energy, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Conservation, Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its ambitious targets. Explain its dual role in energy security (reducing fossil fuel import dependence) and climate change mitigation (decarbonization). Detail opportunities like job creation, export potential, and technological innovation. Address challenges such as high production costs, infrastructure development, water availability, and R&D funding. Conclude with policy recommendations for successful implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's recent ruling on Union-State legislative competence for the principles of cooperative federalism in India. In what ways do judicial pronouncements shape the balance of power within India's federal structure?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—features, federal structure, functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by explaining the concept of federalism and cooperative federalism in India, referencing constitutional provisions like the Concurrent List. Detail the essence of the Supreme Court's ruling and its impact on states' legislative autonomy. Discuss how judicial interpretations, especially on legislative competence and basic structure, act as a check on both Union and State powers, ensuring constitutional balance. Conclude with the importance of judicial activism in upholding federal principles.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in promoting financial inclusion and e-governance. Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and robust data privacy within this expanding digital ecosystem.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures. GS3: Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI, mentioning its core components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker. Discuss its successes in financial inclusion (e.g., direct benefit transfers, digital payments) and e-governance (e.g., online services, reducing corruption). Address challenges such as the digital divide, lack of digital literacy, cybersecurity risks, and data privacy concerns. Suggest measures like universal digital literacy programs, affordable internet access, strong data protection laws, independent regulatory bodies, and localized content to ensure inclusivity and security. Conclude with the vision of a truly digital and inclusive India.

quickBytes.text: India aims for 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen production annually by 2030.
Article 246 of the Indian Constitution deals with the subject matter of laws made by Parliament and by the Legislatures of States.
India's Q2 FY 2025-26 GDP growth reported at 7.2%, driven by manufacturing and services.
ASEAN was founded in 1967 with five member states: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.
A qubit is the basic unit of quantum information, capable of existing in multiple states simultaneously.
MGNREGA guarantees 100 days of wage employment to rural households in India.
India is the world's third-largest generator of e-waste, after China and the USA.
Chabahar Port, located in Iran, is strategically important for India's connectivity to Central Asia.
Lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements are examples of critical minerals essential for EVs and renewable energy.
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India.

GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled 'Dekho Apna Desh' 2.0, an upgraded campaign to promote both domestic and international tourism in India.
• The initiative focuses on showcasing India's diverse cultural heritage, natural beauty, and adventure tourism potential.
• New digital platforms, immersive virtual reality experiences, and collaborations with travel influencers are key components.
• Special emphasis is being placed on developing sustainable tourism practices and promoting local economies in tourist destinations.
• The campaign aims to achieve significant growth in tourist arrivals and boost the hospitality sector, aligning with economic recovery goals.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Key Federalism Ruling
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment clarifying the legislative competence of states versus the Union in matters of concurrent list subjects.
• The ruling stemmed from a dispute over a state law regulating agricultural markets, which was challenged by the Union government.
• The Court emphasized the principle of cooperative federalism, stating that while the Union has overriding powers in certain circumstances, states retain significant autonomy.
• The judgment is expected to have far-reaching implications for future legislative actions concerning subjects like education, forests, and trade.
• Experts suggest the ruling reaffirms the constitutional balance of power and strengthens the federal structure of India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: Role of Supreme Court in interpreting constitutional provisions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the Union-State relations in India.
Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism and its challenges in the Indian context.
Evaluate how judicial pronouncements shape the balance of power between the Union and states.
Examine the significance of legislative competence in maintaining the federal structure.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The 'basic structure doctrine' of the Indian Constitution was propounded by the Supreme Court in the Kesavananda Bharati case in 1973.

GS2.headline: India and ASEAN Deepen Maritime Security Cooperation amidst Regional Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the annual India-ASEAN Maritime Security Dialogue, focusing on enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions centered on joint naval exercises, information sharing, and capacity building to address challenges like piracy, illegal fishing, and maritime terrorism.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law, particularly UNCLOS.
• The dialogue also explored avenues for collaboration in disaster management and humanitarian assistance in the maritime domain.
• This strengthened partnership is seen as crucial for regional stability and countering growing geopolitical complexities in the South China Sea.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.
keyTerms: UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea)
prelimsPoints: Key challenges in maritime security (piracy, illegal fishing).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-ASEAN maritime cooperation for regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its strategic partnership with ASEAN.
Evaluate India's 'Act East' policy in the context of its maritime security objectives.
Examine how adherence to international law like UNCLOS contributes to a rules-based order in the maritime domain.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and ASEAN member states participating in a joint exercise.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

GS2.headline: Flagship Rural Development Schemes Show Positive Impact on Livelihoods
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Rural Development indicated significant progress in key flagship schemes.
• Programs like MGNREGA, Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), and Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) have shown enhanced outreach.
• Data revealed a noticeable improvement in rural infrastructure, employment generation, and women's self-help group empowerment.
• Challenges persist in equitable resource distribution and timely implementation in remote areas, requiring further administrative reforms.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to boosting rural incomes and reducing poverty through integrated development strategies.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Rural Development / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Nodal Ministry for rural development schemes.
Role of Self-Help Groups in women's empowerment.
Key indicators of rural development (employment, infrastructure).
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the impact of flagship rural development schemes on poverty alleviation and livelihood generation in India.
Discuss the role of women's Self-Help Groups in promoting economic empowerment and social change in rural areas.
Examine the administrative and logistical challenges in implementing large-scale rural development programs and suggest solutions.
Analyze how an integrated approach to rural development can lead to sustainable and inclusive growth.
GS2.imageDescription: Rural women participating in a self-help group meeting in a village.
GS2.trivia: MGNREGA guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Emphasizes Defense and Economic Ties
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan held their annual Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to a Special Strategic and Global Partnership.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, including joint exercises, technology transfer, and collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
• Economic ties were also a major agenda, with talks on boosting trade, investment, and supply chain resilience between the two nations.
• Both countries underscored their shared vision for a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and discussed regional security challenges.
• The dialogue also covered cooperation in infrastructure development, clean energy, and people-to-people exchanges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) / The Japan Times

Economic initiatives between India and Japan.
Role of Quad in regional security.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' with Japan in the context of changing geopolitical dynamics.
Discuss the potential for enhanced defense and economic cooperation between India and Japan, particularly in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India-Japan collaboration in critical and emerging technologies.
Examine how bilateral partnerships like India-Japan contribute to regional stability and a rules-based international order.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the largest investors in India, particularly in infrastructure projects like the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail corridor.

GS2.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Expanding, Focus on Inclusivity
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced plans to further expand India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• Initiatives include integrating more government services into the Aadhaar-UPI-DigiLocker stack and promoting digital literacy in rural areas.
• The focus is on ensuring equitable access to digital services and financial inclusion for all segments of society.
• New guidelines for data governance and privacy protection within the DPI framework are also being finalized.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving the vision of a truly digital and inclusive India, leveraging technology for public welfare.
gsSubTopics: E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Components of India's DPI (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker).
Importance of data privacy in digital services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure in promoting financial inclusion and e-governance.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring equitable access to digital services and addressing the digital divide in India.
Evaluate the importance of robust data governance and privacy frameworks for the successful expansion of DPI.
Examine how DPI can be leveraged to improve public service delivery and enhance transparency and accountability.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic showing interconnected digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
GS2.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

GS2.headline: Urban Local Bodies to Undergo Major Reforms for Enhanced Governance
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a comprehensive reform package for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) across the country.
• The reforms aim to enhance the financial autonomy, administrative efficiency, and participatory governance of municipal corporations.
• Key proposals include increasing own-source revenue generation, capacity building for urban planners, and integrating digital solutions for public services.
• The package also emphasizes greater citizen engagement through ward committees and online feedback mechanisms.
• These reforms are critical for addressing the rapid urbanization challenges and improving the quality of life in Indian cities.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

prelimsPoints: Constitutional status of ULBs (74th Amendment Act).
Government initiatives for urban development (e.g., Smart Cities Mission).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of strengthening Urban Local Bodies for effective urban governance and sustainable development in India.
Analyze the proposed reforms package, focusing on its potential to enhance financial autonomy and administrative efficiency of ULBs.
Evaluate the role of citizen participation and digital solutions in improving public service delivery in urban areas.
Examine the challenges in implementing municipal reforms and suggest strategies for successful decentralization of power and finances.
GS2.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with an overlay representing digital governance services.
GS2.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, passed in 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies in India.

GS2.headline: India-GCC Free Trade Agreement Negotiations in Advanced Stages
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have entered their final stages.
• The FTA aims to significantly boost trade and investment flows between India and the six GCC member states.
• Key areas of discussion include tariff reductions, market access for goods and services, and investment protection.
• A successful FTA is expected to provide India with greater energy security and market access, while GCC countries benefit from India's growing economy and skilled workforce.
• Both sides expressed optimism for a swift conclusion, potentially by the end of the year.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.

mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the GCC.
Discuss the potential impact of the FTA on India's energy security, trade balance, and investment landscape.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in negotiating and implementing a comprehensive FTA with a bloc like GCC.
Examine how economic diplomacy through FTAs can strengthen India's geopolitical influence and economic resilience.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states arranged to represent cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The GCC comprises Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focus on Telemedicine
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced the expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to include advanced telemedicine services.
• The initiative aims to connect more healthcare providers and patients through a unified digital platform, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• New features include AI-powered diagnostic support, remote monitoring for chronic diseases, and secure digital prescriptions.
• This expansion is expected to significantly improve access to quality healthcare, reduce geographical barriers, and enhance efficiency.
• Data privacy and security remain paramount, with robust encryption protocols and consent-based data sharing mechanisms.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: National Health Authority (NHA) / Press Information Bureau (PIB)
prelimsPoints: Importance of data privacy in healthcare.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze how the expansion of telemedicine services under ABDM can address the challenges of healthcare access in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the ethical and privacy concerns associated with digital health records and AI-powered diagnostics.
Examine the role of technology and government initiatives in achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor conducting a virtual consultation with a patient via video call.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PMJAY) is the world's largest government-funded healthcare scheme.

GS2.headline: India-Central Asia Summit Focuses on Connectivity and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the 3rd India-Central Asia Summit, bringing together leaders from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
• The summit emphasized strengthening regional connectivity through the Chabahar Port and International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
• Discussions also focused on boosting trade, investment, and cultural exchanges, with agreements signed on energy and pharmaceuticals.
• Security cooperation, particularly in counter-terrorism and regional stability, remained a key agenda item.
• India reiterated its commitment to a long-term strategic partnership with Central Asian nations, leveraging historical ties and shared interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests, Indian diaspora.

Key areas of cooperation (connectivity, trade, security).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Central Asia for India, particularly in terms of connectivity, energy security, and regional stability.
Discuss how initiatives like Chabahar Port and INSTC are crucial for enhancing India's trade and economic engagement with Central Asian countries.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in strengthening security cooperation between India and Central Asian nations, especially in counter-terrorism.
Examine India's 'Connect Central Asia' policy and its role in fostering a multi-faceted partnership.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) route.
GS2.trivia: The International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC) is a 7,200-km long multi-modal network of ship, rail, and road route for moving freight between India, Iran, Azerbaijan, Russia, Central Asia and Europe.

GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education introduced a comprehensive policy framework for the integration of cutting-edge technology into the education system.
• The policy focuses on leveraging AI, virtual reality (VR), and personalized learning platforms to enhance teaching and learning outcomes.
• It mandates digital literacy training for teachers and students across all levels of education, from primary to higher education.
• Initiatives include development of open-source educational resources and deployment of smart classrooms in underserved regions.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Gains Momentum with New Investment Hubs
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced the establishment of three new Green Hydrogen production and export hubs across coastal states.
• This initiative aims to accelerate India's ambitious target of producing 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
• Private sector investment pledges worth over ₹50,000 crores were secured, focusing on electrolyser manufacturing and renewable energy integration.
• The move is expected to significantly boost India's energy security, reduce reliance on fossil fuels, and position the nation as a global leader in green energy exports.
• Skill development programs and R&D grants for hydrogen technologies were also announced to support the ecosystem.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Inflation Remains a Concern
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew by 7.2% in the second quarter (July-September) of FY 2025-26, surpassing market forecasts.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the manufacturing and services sectors, coupled with increased government capital expenditure.
• However, retail inflation, particularly food inflation, continued to remain elevated at 6.8%, putting pressure on household budgets.
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated it would maintain a cautious monetary policy stance, prioritizing inflation control while supporting growth.
• Analysts predict continued economic resilience but highlight the need for structural reforms to address persistent inflationary pressures and ensure equitable growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major Crops - Cropping Patterns in various parts of the country, - Different Types of Irrigation and Irrigation Systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
GS3.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) / RBI
prelimsPoints: Primary drivers of economic growth (manufacturing, services).
Key economic indicators and their calculation (GDP, CPI).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's robust economic growth in Q2 FY 2025-26.
Discuss the challenges posed by persistent inflation and the measures taken by the RBI to control it.
Evaluate the balance between growth and inflation control in India's current economic policy framework.
Suggest structural reforms necessary to sustain economic growth and mitigate inflationary pressures in the long term.
GS3.imageDescription: Infographic showing GDP growth and inflation trends in India.
GS3.trivia: India aims to become a developed economy by 2047, the centenary of its independence.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission: India Makes Strides in Quantum Computing and Communication
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) released a progress report on the National Quantum Mission (NQM), highlighting significant advancements.
• Indian researchers have achieved breakthroughs in developing indigenous quantum processors and secure quantum communication protocols.
• New funding allocations were announced for setting up more Quantum Technology Hubs in premier academic and research institutions.
• The mission aims to propel India into the league of leading nations in quantum technology development, with applications in computing, communication, sensing, and metrology.
• Industry collaborations are being fostered to translate research into commercial applications, creating a robust quantum ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Quantum Mission.
Key areas of quantum technology (computing, communication, sensing).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Quantum Mission for India's technological sovereignty and economic growth.
Analyze the potential applications of quantum computing and communication in various sectors (defense, finance, healthcare).
Examine the challenges in developing a robust quantum technology ecosystem in India, including skill development and infrastructure.
Evaluate how government initiatives like NQM can foster innovation and indigenization in advanced technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract graphic representing quantum bits (qubits) and quantum entanglement.
GS3.trivia: A qubit, the basic unit of quantum information, can exist in multiple states simultaneously, unlike a classical bit which is either 0 or 1.

GS3.headline: Government Proposes Amendments to E-Waste Management Rules for Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change proposed significant amendments to the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, focusing on stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
• The amendments aim to streamline the collection, dismantling, and recycling processes, encouraging a circular economy approach for electronic goods.
• New provisions include mandatory digital manifests for e-waste movement and enhanced penalties for non-compliance by producers and recyclers.
• The move is crucial to tackle the escalating problem of electronic waste, which poses severe environmental and health hazards.
• Stakeholder consultations are underway, with industry bodies and environmental groups providing feedback on the proposed changes.
gsSubTopics: Environmental Pollution and Degradation
Conservation
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: Key provisions of E-Waste Management Rules.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by e-waste generation in India and the necessity of robust management policies.
Analyze how the proposed amendments to E-Waste Management Rules can promote a circular economy and sustainable consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in the Indian context for managing electronic waste.
Suggest measures to improve e-waste collection, recycling infrastructure, and public awareness in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic gadgets representing e-waste.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of e-waste globally, after China and the United States.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework for Climate-Resilient Agriculture Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched a new national policy framework aimed at promoting climate-resilient agriculture.
• The policy emphasizes the adoption of drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and climate-smart farming practices.
• Financial incentives and technological support will be provided to farmers, especially small and marginal farmers, to adapt to changing climate patterns.
• The framework also includes provisions for robust early warning systems for extreme weather events and crop insurance reforms.
• This initiative is critical for ensuring food security and sustaining agricultural productivity in the face of climate change impacts.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
